title: digitaria stricta var. denudata
intro: Digitaria stricta var. denudata, also known as naked finger grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Digitaria stricta var. denudata include reducing inflammation, aiding digestion, and providing relief from skin irritations and infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and can be used externally as a compress or poultice to treat various ailments.
